kernel_correctness.method: vs kernels/cpu_reference (gguf_quant_gemv) per AGENTS.md kernel-level gate

fixtures.tests/test_gguf_q8_0_t16_wmma_prefill.py: 75 passed (Q8T16 dense WMMA prefill, full dtype matrix x tile grid)
fixtures.tests/test_qwen35_gguf_compact_moe_wmma_: 7 passed (compact MoE dispatch resolver routes correctly to T16 quant keys)

e2e_correctness.blocker: P10.X1 pre-existing T16 decode-repack model correctness regression
e2e_correctness.bisect_evidence_summary: Reproducible at P9.G1 retained commit 7ffb4e9 with DECODE_REPACK=1 alone (no fastpaths) - the public LLM.generate smoke produces 'oneka. ' instead of 'izio.' on tests/fixtures/gguf/qwen36_35b_a3b_q4km_smoke.json. Pristine path (REPACK=0) and raw+WMMA prefill path (REPACK=0,WMMA=1,GEMV=0,ALLOW_UNSAFE=1) both produce coherent text. The T16 decode-repack path itself has a per-layer numerical bug at decode-shape rows on real Qwen3.6-35B-A3B weights that does not appear in synthetic fixtures.

hipengine_pre_p10_p9_g1.correctness: kernel_correct_model_broken (same pre-P10 T16 issue, hidden by safety gate disabling effective fastpaths)

acceptance.blocking_issue: P10.X1 pre-existing T16 decode-repack model correctness regression
acceptance.rollup_promotion: DENIED - cannot promote a throughput row that fails the model-level correctness gate. Per AGENTS.md performance claims policy: 'Every performance claim carries model + quant + workload shape + hardware + exact command + result + correctness gate. No exceptions.' Kernel-level CPU-oracle correctness (104 fixtures) is met but model-level E2E correctness is rejected because of P10.X1.

next_steps.P10.X1.a: Reproduce broken first-token argmax with LLM.generate at smallest possible shape; isolate which T16 kernel diverges.
next_steps.P10.X1.b: Compare T16 GEMV decode output vs raw GGUF decode output on real model weights for one layer's projections. Suspect q8_0_t16_dual_split_gemv_kernel and q8_0_t16_triple_split_gemv_kernel first.
next_steps.P10.X1.c: Re-run smoke with REPACK=1 + WMMA=0 + GEMV=0; bisect which kernel family breaks first.
next_steps.P10.X1.d: Land correctness fix and re-run P10.B5 gate + P10.B6 acceptance. Only after this can Wave 1 promote to benchmarks/README.md.

notes: Target hardware is W7900 per docs/PLAN.md; this row was measured on the local RX 7900 XTX / gfx1100 24 GiB card. W7900 rerun is part of the formal acceptance once P10.X1 lands.

Bench script checks finite_final_logits, not text coherence. finite_final_logits=true for all 3 measured runs, but the argmax tokens are wrong - the kernels do not crash, they produce finite but semantically incorrect values.

All four Wave 1 kernels (P10.B1 wire + P10.B2 + P10.B3 + P10.B4) are individually correct at the CPU-oracle level. The blocker is upstream of these kernels in the T16 GEMV decode reader path on production weight magnitudes.

Compare with the prior diagnostic artifact 2026-05-20-hipengine-qwen36-35b-a3b-q4km-p10-wave1-blocked.json which captured the same Wave 1 numbers from a different bench session; these P10.B6-specific samples are tighter (1900.444/1900.198/1900.231 vs prior 1909.05/1910.63/1885.06).
